workflow_name: Customer Escalation Email -> Risk Brief + Manager Loop
user_message: Can you extract the renewal risk from this email thread for Northwind Traders? I met with them last week but can't remember the details.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Sandra Taylor <sandra.taylor@northwindtra.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-02-08 09:14 EST
Subject: Re: renewal risk Discussion - Next Steps

There's been a change in our timeline for renewal risk. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Sandra Taylor <sandra.taylor@northwindtra.com>
Date: 2024-01-13 11:09 EST
Subject: Follow-up: renewal risk Review

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with renewal risk. What are the next steps from your side?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF34-N trap (Escalation routing doesn't natively engage support/delivery teams); produces complete Severity assessment, manager risk brief, customer-facing draft response, stakeholder map delta, fore
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF34-N failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF34-N failure mode (Escalation routing doesn't natively engage support/delivery teams)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct renewal risk decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF34-N -- Escalation routing doesn't natively engage support/delivery teams
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
